items.title: Introducing GPT-5.4 mini and nano | OpenAI
items.author: openai.com
items.snippet: OpenAI has been on an aggressive model release cadence in early 2026. The marquee launch was GPT-5.4 on March 5, 2026, positioned as OpenAI's most powerful general-purpose model for enterprise and professional work. GPT-5.4 integrates coding capabilities from the Codex line, native computer-use for agentic workflows, and supports up to 1M token context windows. It achieves a new state of the art on the GDPval benchmark, matching or exceeding industry professionals in 83% of comparisons vs. 70.9%

items.title: OpenAI on X: Introducing ChatGPT Health
items.author: x.com
items.snippet: OpenAI's @OpenAI Twitter/X account in March 2026 has been active with several major announcements. Most notably, OpenAI announced a large-scale infrastructure investment backed by SoftBank, NVIDIA, and Amazon, framing it as a push to bring AI to more people through expanded compute capacity. The account also promoted the launch of ChatGPT Health, a dedicated space for health conversations that allows users to securely connect medical records and wellness apps, designed to help users navigate med

items.title: Evaluating Chain-of-Thought Monitorability | OpenAI
items.author: openai.com
items.snippet: OpenAI has published several notable research papers and announcements in early 2026. Key scientific research includes 'AI as a Scientific Collaborator' (January 2026), which examines how AI models can assist researchers across disciplines, and a paper on using a GPT-5-driven autonomous lab to optimize cell-free protein synthesis, demonstrating AI-accelerated wet lab science. OpenAI also introduced FrontierScience, a new expert-verified benchmark spanning physics, chemistry, and biology designed
